title: Trends in University Department Preferences in Turkey
text:
Welcome to my/our project page.
Keep an eye on this space to stay updated with my project activities.
(The titles below are provided as examples; please feel free to adjust them as necessary.)

title: Trends in University Department Preferences in Turkey
text:
2.1 Data Source
The data is obtained from the `thestats` package in R, which scrapes data from the YÖK Atlas portal maintained by the Turkish Higher Education Council (YÖK). YÖK Atlas provides detailed statistics on university programs, student preferences, and placement results across Turkey.

title: Trends in University Department Preferences in Turkey
text:
2.2 General Information About Data
The dataset contains 25,652 rows and 198 variables. For this project, 10 key variables were selected:

year: Year the data was announced (2018, 2019, 2020)
type: Type of university (State or Private)
university: Name of the university
department: Name of the department/program
score_type: Entrance exam score type (SAY, EA, SÖZ, DİL)
quota: General quota of the program
placed: Number of students placed
lowest_score: Lowest placement score
highest_score: Highest placement score
num_choices: Number of students who chose this program

title: Trends in University Department Preferences in Turkey
text:
2.3 Reason of Choice
This dataset was selected for the following reasons:

It is directly accessible via R without any manual downloading or web scraping
It covers all universities and departments in Turkey, making it representative at the national level
It enables trend analysis across years and comparison across university types and score categories
The topic has direct relevance to Turkey’s higher education policy and planning

title: Trends in University Department Preferences in Turkey
text:
2.4 Preprocessing
The raw scores dataset from the thestats package contains 198 variables. For this project, 10 key variables were selected and renamed for clarity using the dplyr package. The preprocessing code is as follows:


Show the code
library(thestats)


Zorunlu paket yükleniyor: dplyr



Attaching package: 'dplyr'


The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':

    filter, lag


The following objects are masked from 'package:base':

    intersect, setdiff, setequal, union


Show the code
library(dplyr)

data(scores)

df <- scores |>
  select(year, type, university, department,
         X6, X9, X11, X14, X16, X139) |>
  rename(
    score_type = X6,
    quota = X9,
    placed = X11,
    lowest_score = X14,
    highest_score = X16,
    num_choices = X139
  )

save(df, file = "data.RData")


The processed dataset is stored in .RData format and can be accessed here.
